flashcards.front: What is the set-theoretic notation for the standard set of Natural Numbers (ℕ)?
Provide a counterexample demonstrating that Natural Numbers (ℕ) are not closed under subtraction.
flashcards.back: If a = 3 and b = 5 (both in ℕ), their difference is a - b = -2, which is not in ℕ (it is a negative integer).

flashcards.front: What is the role of the successor function S(n) in Peano Axioms?
flashcards.back: It formally defines the progression of numbers, mapping any natural number n to its unique successor S(n) = n + 1, ensuring the set is infinite.
